etymology.description: 雈(雈) (huán, 'owl') + 臼(臼) (jiù, 'mortar') = originally an owl -> phonetically borrowed for 'old'
etymology.interpretation: 舊(舊) combines the semantic component 雈(雈) representing an owl, and the phonetic component 臼(臼). It was later used for its phonetic value to represent the concept of 'old’ as this word sounded the same as a word meaning 'old'.
etymology.simplification: 舊(舊) was simplified to 旧(舊) by replacing 雈(雈) (huán, 'owl') with 日(日) (rì, 'sun'), which is visually simpler and retains some phonetic similarity.

examples.chinese: 这个旧房子很有历史(這個舊房子很有歷史)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège jiù fángzi hěn yǒu lìshǐ.
examples.english: This old house has a lot of history.

examples.chinese: 他穿着旧鞋子(他穿著舊鞋子)，走了很长的路(走了很長的路)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chuānzhe jiù xiézi, zǒu le hěn cháng de lù.
examples.english: He wore old shoes and walked a long way.

examples.chinese: 旧时光总是让人怀念(舊時光總是讓人懷念)。
examples.pinyin: Jiù shíguāng zǒng shì ràng rén huáiniàn.
